codeReview.summary: This is a RE-RUN after the prior review identified that backend endpoints are missing. The issue was NOT fixed - the backend router.py still only contains user management endpoints (login, dashboard, users). No content management endpoints were added.

issues.description: BLOCKING ISSUE PERSISTS: Backend admin router does not implement content management endpoints. Frontend calls /admin/subjects, /admin/topics, /admin/lessons, /admin/questions, /admin/test-series but these are NOT defined in router.py. Existing endpoints remain only: POST /admin/login, GET /admin/dashboard, GET /admin/users, GET /admin/users/{user_id}, PATCH /admin/users/{user_id}/status. The git diff between f86837d and HEAD shows zero changes to router.py.
BLOCKING ISSUE PERSISTS: No Pydantic schemas defined for AdminSubject, AdminTopic, AdminLesson, AdminQuestion, AdminTestSeries, or their Create/Update DTOs in the admin schemas module.
BLOCKING ISSUE PERSISTS: No service methods defined for content management CRUD operations (create_subject, create_topic, create_lesson, create_question, create_test_series, etc.).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature is frontend-only. The frontend calls POST /admin/upload for MinIO file uploads but /admin/upload does not exist in router.py. The feature cannot function as designed without the backend implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/admin.ts defines uploadFile() calling POST /admin/upload. router.py has no /admin/upload endpoint.

summary: FAIL - The prior blocking issue (missing backend endpoints) was NOT fixed. No changes were made to the backend after the prior review. The frontend implementation exists but cannot work at runtime because the backend API endpoints (/admin/subjects, /admin/topics, /admin/lessons, /admin/questions, /admin/test-series, /admin/upload) do not exist in router.py. This is a full-stack feature that requires both frontend and backend to work.
